title: midnight rich venezuelan sloe-berry tagine
description: A rich and silky tagine dish featuring sloe berries, inspired by Venezuelan cuisine, without onions or garlic. Serve warm and enjoy with crusty bread.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality oil for best flavor

ingredients.name: chicken or vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: use low-sodium broth for best flavor

ingredients.name: ground cumin
ingredients.notes: use freshly ground cumin for best flavor

ingredients.name: ground coriander
ingredients.notes: use freshly ground coriander for best flavor

ingredients.name: paprika
ingredients.notes: use sweet or smoked paprika for best flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use flaky sea salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: use freshly ground pepper for best flavor

ingredients.name: carrots
ingredients.notes: use baby carrots for best flavor

ingredients.name: celery
ingredients.notes: use fresh celery for best flavor

ingredients.name: red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: use sweet or hot bell pepper for best flavor

ingredients.name: quinoa or rice
ingredients.notes: use cooked quinoa or rice for serving

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the mirepoix (chopped carrots, celery, and red bell pepper) and cook until tender, about 8 minutes.
Add the sloe berries, broth, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 20-25 minutes or until the sloe berries have broken down and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the cooked quinoa or rice and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Serve the tagine warm, garnished with fresh herbs or a dollop of yogurt, if desired.
